text: Writing clean code is essential for any software developer. Readable code makes it easier for others to understand and modify your work. Use clear naming conventions for variables, functions, and classes; names should describe what they do without extra comments. Functions should be short and focused on a single task, making your program modular and simpler to test. This approach improves maintainability, allowing you to update or fix your code quickly. Remember, coding for humans first benefits everyone, including your future self.
